lines.target: We worked hard yesterday, and today we must look at something new.
lines.base: Ayer trabajamos mucho y hoy debemos considerar algo nuevo.
lines.pronunciation: .. uérkt haard... — 2 ... lukt... lissénd... — 10 ... wos... uér. — 13 ... uos kuait.. — 14 ... houpt...

lines.target: You looked at the lesson and listened to the records (or tapes), so now you are ready to learn the past tense.
lines.base: Viste la lección y escuchaste los discos (o cintas), así que ahora estás preparado para aprender el pasado.

lines.target: It is very simple. You add “ed” to the infinitive if it ends in a consonant;
lines.base: Es muy simple. Se añade «-ed» al infinitivo si termina en consonante.

lines.target: and simply “-d” to the infinitive ending in “e”.
For example: to look, I looked; to work, he worked;
lines.base: Por ejemplo: mirar -> miré; trabajar -> trabajé.

lines.target: To like, they liked; to smile, we smiled.
There are, of course, some irregular verbs, but they are not too complicated.
lines.base: Hay verbos irregulares pero no son complicados.

lines.target: Let's look at our old friends “ to be” and “to have”.
“To have ” is extremely simple. It becomes “ had”: you had, he had. (N.1)
“To be ” has two forms: I was, he was; and “ you were, they were”.
lines.base: Ser tiene dos formas: was / were.

lines.target: You see how simple it is!
We had dinner at eight o'clock, John and Peter were there.
lines.base: Cenamos a las ocho; John y Peter estaban allí.

lines.target: I had a cold last week and I was quite ill.
lines.base: La semana pasada tuve un constipado y estuve bastante enferma.

lines.target: We hoped to see her but she was busy.

gap_candidates.target: We worked hard yesterday, and today we must look at something new. (1)
It is very simple. You add “ed” to the infinitive if it ends in a consonant;
To like, they liked; to smile, we smiled.
“To have ” is extremely simple. It becomes “ had”: you had, he had. (N.1)
You see how simple it is!
I had a cold last week and I was quite ill.
We hoped to see her but she was busy.
